title: clematis alpina var. koreana
intro: Clematis alpina var. koreana, also known as Korean alpine clematis, is a deciduous perennial climbing plant native to Korea and parts of Asia, characterized by its delicate white or pale blue flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Clematis alpina var. koreana include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
